hints.text: Plates: long white, **blue strips both sides** (left more visible) — Italy has equal strips + shorter front plate
French: é à è â ê î ô û, œ, ç; **-ux** suffix only French; street = **rue**
**French bollard**: round white, **pointed top**, reflector band all around (Scotland's is blunt-topped)
**Waffle/ladder poles** (step indents; also Spain) + small **blue plates** on poles (France-unique) + diamond "French poletops"
**Yellow "D" road-number signs** = France only; town-entry signs red border w/ thin white outline
Chevrons **white-on-blue** (only France + Spain; Spain uses only 2 or 4 arrows)
Middle-line dashes = **two offset blocks** → France; blue house numbers (Italy rarely blue)
Peugeot / Renault / Citroën ≈ half of all cars; smallcam (most of W. Europe)
**Postal code first 2 digits = department number** (alphabetical); also inside the right blue plate strip
Phone: **01 = Île-de-France**, 02–05 run clockwise from the NW
**Department logos** on bus stops / km markers / signbacks → identify department
Stone houses, gable roofs w/ orange tiles (S) or black slate (N); very diverse regional architecture
Regional languages appear (Breton NW, Alsatian E, Occitan/Basque/Catalan S)
